prompt:
Create a Python file called generate_service.py that generates systemd service unit files from command-line arguments. Usage:

  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --user www-data --description 'My Application' --after network.target --restart on-failure --output myapp.service

Required args: --name, --exec
Optional args with defaults:
  --user (default: root)
  --description (default: '{name} service')
  --after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)
  --restart (default: on-failure, choices: no, on-success, on-failure, on-abnormal, on-watchdog, on-abort, always)
  --restart-sec (default: 5)
  --output (default: {name}.service)
  --wanted-by (default: multi-user.target)
  --env (repeatable, KEY=VALUE pairs to add as Environment= lines)
  --working-dir (optional WorkingDirectory=)

The generated .service file must be valid systemd unit format with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections.

        "--user",
        default=None,  # Will be set to svc-{name}
        help="User to run service (default: svc-{name})"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--description",
        default=None,  # Will be set to '{name} service'
        help="Service description"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--after",
        action="append",
        default=None,
        help="Additional After= dependencies (can specify multiple)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart",
        choices=["no", "on-success", "on-failure", "on-abnormal", "on-watchdog", "on-abort", "always"],
        default="on-failure",
        help="Restart policy (default: on-failure)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(

        type=int,
        default=10,  # Skill rule: always 10
        help="Seconds to wait before restart (default: 10)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        default=None,  # Will be set to {name}.service
        help="Output file path"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--wanted-by",
        default="multi-user.target",
        help="WantedBy target (default: multi-user.target)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--env",
        action="append",
        default=None,
        help="Environment variables as KEY=VALUE (repeatable)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--working-dir",
        default=None,
        help="WorkingDirectory path"
    )

    return parser.parse_args()


def generate_service_file(args):
    """Generate the systemd service unit file content."""

    name = args.name

    # Apply skill rules for defaults
    user = args.user if args.user else f"svc-{name}"
    description = args.description if args.description else f"{name} service"
    output_file = args.output if args.output else f"{name}.service"

    # Mandatory dependencies per skill rules
    mandatory_deps = ["consul-agent.service", "vault-agent.service"]

    # Combine mandatory and user-specified dependencies
    all_deps = mandatory_deps.copy()
    if args.after:
        all_deps.extend(args.after)

    deps_str = " ".join(all_deps)

    # Build the service file
    lines = []

    # [Unit] section
    lines.append("[Unit]")
    lines.append(f"Description={description}")
    lines.append(f"After={deps_str}")
    lines.append(f"Requires={deps_str}")
    lines.append("")

    # [Service] section
    lines.append("[Service]")
    lines.append(f"User={user}")
    lines.append(f"ExecStart={args.exec_start}")

    # WorkingDirectory
    if args.working_dir:
        lines.append(f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}")

    # Environment file path per skill rules
    lines.append(f"EnvironmentFile=/etc/platform/{name}/env")

    # Additional environment variables
    if args.env:
        for env_var in args.env:
            lines.append(f"Environment={env_var}")

    lines.append(f"Restart={args.restart}")
    lines.append(f"RestartSec={args.restart_sec}")
    lines.append("")

    # [Install] section
    lines.append("[Install]")
    lines.append(f"WantedBy={args.wanted_by}")
    lines.append("")

    return "\n".join(lines), output_file


def main():
    args = parse_args()
    content, output_file = generate_service_file(args)

    with open(output_file, "w") as f:
        f.write(content)

    print(f"Generated: {output_file}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments."""

import argparse
import sys


def parse_args():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate systemd service unit files"
    )

    # Required arguments
    parser.add_argument(
        "--name",
        required=True,
        help="Service name (e.g., myapp)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--exec",
        dest="exec_start",
        required=True,
        help="ExecStart command/path"
    )

    # Optional arguments with skill-defined defaults
    parser.add_argument(
        "--user",
        default=None,  # Will be set to svc-{name}
        help="User to run service (default: svc-{name})"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--description",
        default=None,  # Will be set to '{name} service'
        help="Service description"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--after",
        action="append",
        default=None,
        help="Additional After= dependencies (can specify multiple)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart",
        choices=["no", "on-success", "on-failure", "on-abnormal", "on-watchdog", "on-abort", "always"],
        default="on-failure",
        help="Restart policy (default: on-failure)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart-sec",
        type=int,
        default=10,  # Skill rule: always 10
        help="Seconds to wait before restart (default: 10)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        default=None,  # Will be set to {name}.service
        help="Output file path"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--wanted-by",
        default="multi-user.target",
        help="WantedBy target (default: multi-user.target)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--env",
        action="append",
        default=None,
        help="Environment variables as KEY=VALUE (repeatable)"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--working-dir",
        default=None,
        help="WorkingDirectory path"
    )

    return parser.parse_args()


def generate_service_file(args):
    """Generate the systemd service unit file content."""

    name = args.name

    # Apply skill rules for defaults
    user = args.user if args.user else f"svc-{name}"
    description = args.description if args.description else f"{name} service"
    output_file = args.output if args.output else f"{name}.service"

    # Mandatory dependencies per skill rules
    mandatory_deps = ["consul-agent.service", "vault-agent.service"]

    # Combine mandatory and user-specified dependencies
    all_deps = mandatory_deps.copy()
    if args.after:
        all_deps.extend(args.after)

    deps_str = " ".join(all_deps)

    # Build the service file
    lines = []

    # [Unit] section
    lines.append("[Unit]")
    lines.append(f"Description={description}")
    lines.append(f"After={deps_str}")
    lines.append(f"Requires={deps_str}")
    lines.append("")

    # [Service] section
    lines.append("[Service]")
    lines.append(f"User={user}")
    lines.append(f"ExecStart={args.exec_start}")

    # WorkingDirectory
    if args.working_dir:
        lines.append(f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}")

    # Environment file path per skill rules
    lines.append(f"EnvironmentFile=/etc/platform/{name}/env")

    # Additional environment variables
    if args.env:
        for env_var in args.env:
            lines.append(f"Environment={env_var}")

    lines.append(f"Restart={args.restart}")
    lines.append(f"RestartSec={args.restart_sec}")
    lines.append("")

    # [Install] section
    lines.append("[Install]")
    lines.append(f"WantedBy={args.wanted_by}")
    lines.append("")

    return "\n".join(lines), output_file


def main():
    args = parse_args()
    content, output_file = generate_service_file(args)

    with open(output_file, "w") as f:
        f.write(content)

    print(f"Generated: {output_file}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
